title: Why pedestrians do not constantly collide with each other
description: High-density pedestrian flow experiments and physics-based modelling revealed that collision avoidance emerges from rapid gaze-direction detection and micro-trajectory adjustments happening ~0.5 s before impact. The study found that removing this anticipatory step (as can occur with phone distraction) dramatically increases collision rates — aligning with real-world observations about phone usage and pedestrian accidents.
